If  the  said  causes  are  not  removed,  within  48  hours  after  the  date  of  written  notice  from  Contractor, Contractor may rectify the same at Subcontractor's expense. Contractor may offset against any sums due Subcontractor hereunder the  amount  of  any  liquidated  or  unliquidated  obligation  of  Subcontractor  to  Contractor,  whether  or  not  arising  out  of  this subcontract. Subcontractor agrees to be bound by all the provisions of the Prime Contract, including but not limited to, provisions relating  to  quantities,  measurement  and  payment,  change  orders,  extra  work,  variations  in  plans  and/or  site  conditions,  time extensions and claims. Subcontractor agrees that it will present Contractor with written notice of any event or claim for which it seeks  compensation within forty-eight  hours  of  the  event  occurrence,  so  that  Contractor  can  review  and  mitigate  the  impacts. Subcontractor's failure to comply with this notice requirement shall constitute a waiver and release by Subcontractor of any and all rights to pursue a claim for time or costs. Contractor agrees that it will present to Owner any reasonable claim for payment or time extension which Subcontractor in good faith chooses to submit, provided that Subcontractor prepares all notices in a proper manner sufficiently in advance of the time of notice to permit Contractor to submit the notice to Owner in accordance with the requirements  of  the  Prime  Contract,  and  Subcontractor  prepares  the  documentation  required,  and  complies  with  all  other requirements  of  the  Prime  Contract,  and  does  all  things  necessary  to  enable  Contractor  to  present  Subcontractor's  claims. Contractor agrees to reasonably cooperate with Subcontractor in presenting Subcontractor's claims, and Subcontractor agrees to cooperate with Contractor and all other subcontractors of Contractor in presenting all claims. Contractor's receipt of payment or any time extension from Owner shall be a condition precedent to any obligation of Contractor to Subcontractor with respect to any such claims presented, and Subcontractor may not recover more from Contractor than the amount or relief which Contractor recovers  from  Owner  with  respect  to  any  Subcontractor  claim.  Subcontractor  agrees  to  be  bound  by  the  terms  of  the  Prime Contract with respect to any and all claims. All claims which Subcontractor has or wishes to assert against Contractor must be presented in writing to Contractor not later than two (2) days after Subcontractor is aware or should be aware that a claim will or does exist, or such longer time as may be required by law, even though the exact nature of the claim and the amount of the claim may not be determinable at that time. The exact nature of the claim and the amount of the claim must be presented to Contractor in writing as soon thereafter as Subcontractor has or should have such information. Subcontractor hereby waives all claims not presented  as  provided  herein.  Any  default  by  Subcontractor  under  this  Subcontract  shall  constitute  a  default  under  any  other subcontract  or  agreement  between  Subcontractor  and  Contractor.  Similarly,  any  default  by  Subcontractor  under  any  other subcontract or agreement with Contractor shall constitute a default under this Subcontract, entitling Contractor to exercise any and all  remedies available under this Subcontract, including but not limited to termination, withholding of payment, and pursuit of damages.
